content.description:
# Doc AST: Parity Roadmap

**Status:** Assessment (March 2026)

An analysis of what the `:doc` AST notes (on the `doc-ast-editor` branch) need before they can replace standard markdown notes for daily use. The doc-ast architecture is stronger — unified AST, expressions alongside data, no mini-languages — but has practical editing gaps.

See [Transparency & Conceptual Uniformity](item://c0c0c0c0-0093-0000-0000-000000000000) for the design vision. See [Inline Expression Evaluation](item://c0c0c0c0-005d-0000-0000-000000000000) for the `:transclusion`/`:field-ref` unification proposal. See [Computed Fields vs Transclusions: Gap Analysis](item://c0c0c0c0-0075-0000-0000-000000000000) for the earlier analysis of what transclusions provide.

## What Standard Notes Have

Markdown notes on master provide:

- Markdown body editing (CodeMirror)
- `{{field-name}}` references resolved inline with dashed underline and click-to-scroll
- Computed fields (`content.computed`) with Hob formulas
- Static fields editing
- Field summary (formula + value + provenance links)
- Markdown transclusions (`![](item://id)`) with collapsible `<details>` chrome
- Region/line/symbol extraction (`?region=`, `?lines=`, `?symbol=`)
- Query blocks (` ```query ``` `) with arbitrary JS
- Cross-item reactivity (re-render when referenced items change)
- Error sentinels (red `ERR` spans with tooltip)
- Functional transclusions (computed fields returning DOM)
- Tags and name editing

## What Doc AST Notes Have

The `doc-ast-editor` branch provides:

- Per-block contenteditable prose editor with DOM↔AST sync
- `:doc` AST with `:field-ref`, `:transclusion`, `:item-ref` inline nodes
- Block-level Hob expression evaluation (query blocks via `>>>` shortcut)
- Autocomplete: `@` (item-ref), `{{` (field-ref), `@@` (transclusion)
- Markdown shortcuts (`# ` for headings, `-`/`*` for lists, `>` for blockquotes, `---` for hr)
- Eval block editing via structural editor in edit mode
- Path-based block addressing for nested editing (lists, blockquotes)
- Editable text within `:li` and `:blockquote` children
- Provenance data attributes on all rendered elements
- Tags, name, static fields, computed fields editing (via generic-view)
- Field summary with provenance (via generic-view)
- Readonly view with full AST rendering ([field-view-doc](item://d0d0d0d0-0003-0000-0000-000000000000))
- Markdown-to-AST converter ([markdown-to-doc-ast](item://d0d0d0d0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000))

## Tier 1: Must-Have for Parity

These block daily use without them.

### 1. Structural list operations

**Update (implemented):** All core list operations are now working in the prose editor:
- Enter inside `:li` → splits at cursor, creates new `:li` with after-cursor content
- Enter on empty `:li` → exits list (lifts to `:p`; handles first/last/middle correctly, splitting the list if needed)
- Backspace at start of first `:li` → lifts content out of list as `:p`
- Backspace at start of non-first `:li` → merges with previous `:li`'s last leaf block
- Delete at end of last leaf in `:li` → merges with next `:li`'s first leaf block
- Tab → indents `:li` under previous sibling (appends to existing nested list or creates new one)
- Shift+Tab in nested list → moves `:li` to outer list
- Shift+Tab at top level → lifts `:li` out as `:p` (same as empty-Enter exit)

List creation via existing markdown shortcuts (`- `/`* `/`+ `/`1. ` + space on a top-level paragraph). Implemented via `getListContext()` helper that detects `:p` → `:li` → `:ul`/`:ol` nesting, plus `liftListItem()` shared logic for first/last/middle list splitting.

### 2. Link insertion (Ctrl+K)

**Gap:** No way to insert a URL link from the prose editor. Standard notes get this from markdown syntax (`[text](url)`). The doc AST has `:link` nodes and the readonly renderer handles them, but the editor has no insertion gesture.

**Scope:** Intercept Ctrl+K → prompt for URL (simple input or inline popup) → wrap selected text in `:link` node, or insert a new `:link` node if no selection.

**Effort:** Small. The pattern is established by `@`/`{{`/`@@` autocomplete — this is simpler since there's no search involved.

### 3. Non-editable block management

**Gap:** Horizontal rules, code blocks, and eval blocks are non-editable islands. If you create one accidentally (e.g., typing `---`), there may not be an obvious way to remove it.

**Scope:** Verify existing insertion gap mechanism and keyboard shortcuts handle this. If not: clicking a non-editable block selects it (visual highlight), then Delete/Backspace removes it.

**Effort:** Small. May already partially work via existing block management.

**Update (implemented):** Block management is now complete. Non-editable blocks (hr, code blocks, eval blocks) get keyboard shortcuts: Backspace/Delete removes them, Enter inserts a paragraph after. Insertion gaps appear between adjacent non-editable blocks on hover, allowing paragraph insertion. Arrow keys navigate into/out-of non-editable blocks.

## Tier 2: Architectural Unification

What makes doc-ast notes *better*, not just equal.

### 4. Inline expression evaluation

**Gap:** `:field-ref` and `:transclusion` are hardcoded renderer special cases. The [Inline Expression Evaluation](item://c0c0c0c0-005d-0000-0000-000000000000) proposal says they should be standard Hob `get-field` calls — the renderer should dispatch any non-keyword inline node to the evaluator, just as it already does at block level.

**Why now:** This is the single change that unifies the inline story. Once inline expressions work:
- `:transclusion` → `(get-field @item-id :description)` — no special-casing
- `:field-ref` → `(get-field :self :field-name)` — no special-casing
- Any Hob expression works inline — `(if condition "yes" "no")`, user-defined macros, etc.
- The `@@` and `{{` autocomplete just insert different expression shapes

**Scope:**
1. Add evaluator fallback to `renderInlineNode` (matching what `renderNode` already does for blocks)
2. Update `@@` autocomplete to insert `["get-field", "@item-id", ":field"]` instead of `[:transclusion, ...]`
3. Update `{{` autocomplete to insert `["get-field", ":self", ":field"]` instead of `[:field-ref, ...]`
4. Keep the old node types working during migration (renderer handles them as a compatibility path)

**Effort:** Medium. The block-level pattern already exists — this mirrors it for inline.

### 5. `transclude` function with attribution chrome

**Gap:** Standard notes render transclusions in collapsible `<details>` with "Transcluded from: Name ↗" headers. Doc AST transclusions render inline with no source attribution. For block-level transclusions (embedding a paragraph from another note), attribution matters.

**Scope:** Implement `transclude` as a Hob function returning `{_hobType: "transclusion", value, sourceId, sourceField, chrome}`. The renderer detects this wrapper and generates chrome:
- `:full` — collapsible container with header (for block-level)
- `:subtle` — small attribution link (for inline)
- `:none` — no wrapper

**Depends on:** #4 (inline expression evaluation) — `transclude` is just a function, not a special node type.

**Effort:** Medium.

## Tier 3: Polish and Extended Capabilities

### 6. Error display for inline expressions

**Gap:** Standard notes show red `ERR` spans with tooltips for failed computed fields. Doc AST field-ref rendering needs parity with the sentinel pattern (clickable, tooltip with message, matches the `computed-field-error` sentinel).

**Scope:** Audit `renderInlineNode` error paths. Ensure all inline expression failures produce styled error spans with `data-provenance-*` attributes, tooltip with error message, consistent with the standard note ERR display.

**Effort:** Small.

### 7. Cross-block selection

**Gap:** Can't select text spanning multiple paragraphs. Per-block contenteditable means the browser's native selection stops at block boundaries.

**Scope:** Architecturally harder — requires either a document-level selection overlay or merging to a single contenteditable region. Consider a pragmatic middle ground: detect multi-block selection via mouseup/keydown with Shift, compute the AST range, handle delete/copy at the AST level.

**Effort:** Large. Defer unless it proves to be constant friction during real use.

### 8. Stdlib extraction helpers

**Gap:** Standard markdown transclusions support `?region=name`, `?lines=10-20`, `?symbol=fnName`. No Hob equivalents exist.

**Scope:** Add stdlib functions: `extract-region`, `extract-lines`, `extract-symbol`. These compose with `get-field` and `transclude`. Each is independently useful beyond transclusion.

**Effort:** Small per function. Add incrementally as needs arise.

## Recommended Sequence

| Order | Item | Rationale |
|-------|------|-----------|
| **1** | Structural list operations (#1) | Biggest daily friction — every note with a list hits this |
| **2** | Link insertion (#2) + block management (#3) | Small wins that close obvious editing gaps |
| **3** | Inline expression evaluation (#4) | Architectural unification that makes everything downstream cleaner |
| **4** | Error display audit (#6) | Small, ensures robustness before broader adoption |
| **5** | `transclude` with chrome (#5) | Completes the transclusion story with attribution |
| **6** | Stdlib helpers (#8) | As needed, driven by real note conversion |

Items 1–4 achieve functional parity. Items 5–6 make doc-ast notes strictly superior to standard notes. Cross-block selection (#7) can wait — it's the hardest and least likely to block real use.

## Merge Strategy

Merge to master after items 1–4, with doc-ast views available as **alternative views** for the note type (not replacing the markdown views). This enables daily use of doc-ast notes while keeping markdown as a fallback. The [markdown-to-doc-ast](item://d0d0d0d0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) converter handles migration of existing notes.

Once confidence builds from real use, make the doc views the default for new notes. The markdown views remain available indefinitely — notes already written in markdown continue to work.

## References

- [Transparency & Conceptual Uniformity](item://c0c0c0c0-0093-0000-0000-000000000000) — The unified AST design vision
- [Doc AST: Inline Expression Evaluation](item://c0c0c0c0-005d-0000-0000-000000000000) — Replacing `:field-ref`/`:transclusion` with `get-field` calls
- [Computed Fields vs Transclusions: Gap Analysis](item://c0c0c0c0-0075-0000-0000-000000000000) — What transclusions provide vs computed fields
- [Structured Notes](item://c0c0c0c0-0073-0000-0000-000000000000) — Computed fields merged into the note type
- [Reactive Computed Fields](item://c0c0c0c0-0074-0000-0000-000000000000) — The underlying mechanism
- [field-view-doc](item://d0d0d0d0-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) — Readonly `:doc` AST renderer
- [field-view-doc-editable](item://d0d0d0d0-0007-0000-0000-000000000000) — Prose editor for `:doc` AST
- [markdown-to-doc-ast](item://d0d0d0d0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) — Migration converter
